summary: Das, Draganić, and Steiner (arXiv:2111.05723, JCTB 2023) resolved the asymptotic behavior of f(H,q) by proving f(H,q) ≤ (21/2)qn + 8n + 14q for subcubic H on n vertices, and showing this bound is optimal up to a constant multiplicative factor. This settles the open problem: the asymptotic order of f(H,q) is linear in qn. A 2025 follow-up by Hou and Wang (arXiv:2510.05697) studies the related function s_q(H) in complete-graph subdivisions and achieves further improvements for special cases.

since_posted.title: Tight bounds for divisible subdivisions
since_posted.authors: Shagnik Das, Nemanja Draganić, Raphael Steiner
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves f(H,q) ≤ (21/2)qn + 8n + 14q for subcubic H on n vertices and shows this is optimal up to a constant factor, resolving the asymptotic behavior of f(H,q) posed in arXiv:2012.05112.

since_posted.title: Divisible subdivisions of graphs in subdivisions of complete graphs
since_posted.claim: Studies the related function s_q(H) (minimum complete-graph order for a q-divisible subdivision of H), proving improved upper bounds and resolving the case q=2 completely for 5-degenerate graphs and trees.

search_queries: divisible subdivisions f(H,q) minor graph Alon Krivelevich arxiv 2012.05112
subdivision divisible path length modulo graph minor bounds 2022 2023 2024
notes: The key resolution is arXiv:2111.05723 (Das–Draganić–Steiner), which appeared on arXiv in November 2021 (after the June 2021 journal publication of the source paper) and was published in JCTB in 2023. The bound f(H,q) = Θ(qn) is tight up to constants, so the asymptotic question is answered. The 2025 paper (arXiv:2510.05697) addresses a variant with complete-graph subdivisions rather than K_f-minors.
